<[silence:3400ms]>Tiny prefix. <[silence:1300ms]> Means "inside" or "within."<[silence:1300ms]>"In" comes from Latin, where it meant into—like stepping into a room.<[silence:700ms]>Now it hides in words, changing their meaning.<[silence:1300ms]>Think of "visible" (able to be seen) vs. "invisible" (in + visible = not seen).<[silence:700ms]>Or "flame" (fire) vs. "inflame" (in + flame = set on fire inside).<[silence:1300ms]>It’s a silent switch.<[silence:700ms]>—"Active" becomes "inactive" (turned off).
—"Correct" becomes "incorrect" (wrong).
—"Sane" becomes "insane" (unbalanced mind).<[silence:1300ms]>Even numbers use it:<[silence:700ms]>"inch" (measurement) holds in for "one-twelfth of a foot."<[silence:700ms]>Grammar too:<[silence:700ms]>"in the box" (location), "in love" (state of being).<[silence:2000ms]>Three related subjects are word roots, language shifts, and out prefixes.<[silence:1200ms]>
clean_story:
Tiny prefix.  Means "inside" or "within. ""In" comes from Latin, where it meant into—like stepping into a room. Now it hides in words, changing their meaning. Think of "visible" (able to be seen) vs. "invisible" (in + visible = not seen). Or "flame" (fire) vs. "inflame" (in + flame = set on fire inside). It’s a silent switch. —"Active" becomes "inactive" (turned off).
—"Correct" becomes "incorrect" (wrong).
—"Sane" becomes "insane" (unbalanced mind). Even numbers use it:"inch" (measurement) holds in for "one-twelfth of a foot. "Grammar too:"in the box" (location), "in love" (state of being). Three related subjects are word roots, language shifts, and out prefixes.
